title: Q1 Product Launch Retrospective
body: The Q1 2026 launch exceeded activation targets by 18%. Onboarding drop-off remained highest at step 3 (payment). Follow-up experiments planned for Q2.

title: Feature Prioritization Framework v2
body: Revised RICE scoring to weight reach by segment-level ARR. Eng estimates now require a confidence interval rather than a single number.

title: Mobile-First Navigation Rewrite Proposal
body: Replacing sidebar with bottom-tab pattern on mobile. Early user tests show 22% faster task completion on the most common flows.

title: Dark Mode Adoption Metrics
body: Dark mode now default for 43% of active users. Correlates weakly with session length (r=0.12) — not a retention driver on its own.

title: Pricing Experiment Results — Tier Consolidation
body: Consolidating Pro and Team into a single tier reduced checkout friction. Revenue neutral after 6 weeks; churn dropped 4%.

title: Competitor Teardown: Acme Labs
body: Acme Labs shipped a hosted workflow builder in Feb 2026. Differentiator is tight GitHub integration; weakness is no offline story.

title: Pricing Comparison Q1 2026
body: Our mid-tier pricing sits 12% below the median of 8 tracked competitors. Free tier is the most generous on API quota.

title: Open-Source Alternatives Landscape
body: Three OSS alternatives reached >5k stars in Q1. None ship a managed cloud offering; deployment remains the primary adoption barrier.

title: Competitor Hiring Signals
body: Competitor X posted 14 infra roles in the last 60 days, suggesting a capacity expansion rather than a product pivot.

title: Acquisition Rumor: Beta Inc -> Gamma Corp
body: Unconfirmed rumor of Beta Inc acquisition by Gamma Corp. If true, would concentrate 31% of the SMB segment under one vendor.

title: Enterprise Customer Interviews Summary (n=12)
body: Top request: role-based access control with fine-grained scopes. Second: SAML SSO. Third: audit log export to S3.

title: Churn Analysis — February Cohort
body: Highest-churn segment is self-serve customers under 10 seats. Primary driver: unclear value at seat 3-5 expansion points.

title: NPS Survey Results Q1
body: NPS rose from 38 to 44. Strongest lift in the developer persona; drag from ops persona (concerns around alerting reliability).

title: Design Partner Program Status
body: Six active design partners; four are shipping features in production. Two are stalled pending RBAC (see doc-011).

title: Customer Advisory Board Notes — March
body: CAB members flagged compliance (SOC2 Type II) as a deal-gating concern for regulated industries. Timeline asked: by Q3 2026.

title: Observability Stack Migration Plan
body: Moving from vendor A to self-hosted OpenTelemetry + Grafana. Q2 target: full trace coverage for the agent runtime path.

title: Database Sharding Readiness Review
body: Primary Postgres cluster at 68% capacity. Sharding POC unblocked; horizontal scale strategy selected (range partition by tenant).

title: CI Pipeline Latency Reduction
body: P50 CI time dropped from 14min to 6min after test parallelization. Flaky test quarantine lane reduced reruns by 60%.

title: Model Serving Cost Analysis
body: Inference spend up 41% QoQ, driven by agent workloads. Cache-hit rate on embedding recomputation only 22% — major savings lever.

title: Disaster Recovery Drill Report
body: Full region failover completed in 11 minutes (target: <15). One runbook gap: DNS propagation timing was under-documented.
